news.summary: In a recent announcement on May 15, 2025, in Linköping, Sweden and Mississauga, ON, a global medical imaging IT and cybersecurity corporation, Sectra (STO: SECT B), revealed that Guelph General Hospital in Canada is set to become the inaugural healthcare facility in the nation to integrate Sectra's digital pathology module into its existing system. This strategic move aims to enhance the hospital's diagnostic capabilities and streamline pathology processes.
news.headline: Guelph General Hospital in Canada becomes first in country to add Sectra's digital pathology module for improved patient care.

news.summary: In Osaka, Japan on May 15, 2025, the Chinese solar term "Grain Buds" signals the start of a summer fashion extravaganza. The renowned "Made in Shanghai" fashion exhibition and trade event is set to make its debut in Umeda, Osaka from May 21st to 25th. The event will showcase over 1000 fashion pieces, highlighting the latest trends and designs from Shanghai's vibrant fashion scene. The event is expected to attract a large audience, including industry professionals and fashion enthusiasts, eager to witness the cultural exchange between Shanghai and Japan.
news.headline: "Made-in-Shanghai" fashion event to land in Osaka from May 21-25, showcasing Shanghai's fashion ecosystem.

news.summary: The global industrial vacuum cleaner market is expected to reach approximately USD 5.1 billion in 2024 and projected to grow to an estimated USD 8.8 billion by 2034, at a CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate) of 5.6% from 2025 to 2034. Factors driving this growth include automation, workplace safety and hygiene standards, and rising global construction activity. Key trends reshaping the industry include automation-enabled cleaning systems, portable and versatile equipment, and enhanced filtration systems for healthier workspaces. Manufacturers are also focusing on sustainability and eco-friendly innovation. The market is driven by stringent hygiene and workplace safety regulations, and technological advancements in smarter, energy-efficient vacuum systems. However, high upfront investment costs are a restraint, particularly for small and medium-sized enterprises. The Wet & Dry vacuum cleaner segment dominated the market in 2023, and electric-powered industrial vacuum cleaners held the largest share due to their efficiency and compatibility with smart technologies. In terms of regional insights, Europe led the global market share in 2023.
news.headline: Global industrial vacuum cleaner market is projected to reach USD 8.8 billion by 2034 with a CAGR of 5.6%. Major drivers include automation, workplace safety, and hygiene focus. Key trends are automation-enabled cleaning systems, portable equipment, advanced filtration, and eco-friendly innovations. Market dynamics show growth driven by hygiene regulations and technological advancements. Hindered by high upfront costs for SMEs. Wet & Dry vacuum cleaners dominated with electric-powered cleaners leading. Europe leads in market share due to mature industrial ecosystem.

news.summary: Bermuda-based Paratus Energy Services Ltd. (ticker: PLSV) is set to unveil its unaudited interim financial results for the first quarter of 2025 on May 28, 2025, around 07:00 CEST. The Company plans to accompany the earnings release with an audio webcast.
news.headline: Paratus Energy Services (PLSV) to unveil Q1 2025 financial results on May 28, 2025. Audio webcast scheduled.

news.summary: A recent report states that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a new low of 3.8%, marking a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4.5%. The report also highlights a record increase in job creation, with over 250,000 new jobs added in the past quarter. Additionally, the report shows a growth in the GDP by 4.2%, surpassing initial estimates. Experts attribute this positive trend to various factors including increased consumer spending and business investments. The government officials have expressed optimism about the country's economic outlook moving forward.
news.headline: Gold prices drop as bets on Fed rate cut decline, safe-haven demand lessens.

news.summary: Chinese darknet marketplace Haowang Guarantee, suspected of facilitating cybercrime and crypto scams, was shut down by Telegram by banning associated accounts on May 13, leading to the marketplace's closure. The marketplace had facilitated around $27 billion in illicit transactions using Tether stablecoin, according to Elliptic. Additionally, the wider Huione Group facilitated over $98 billion in crypto transactions. The marketplace provided services for crypto scammers, including money laundering and selling stolen personal data, deepfake software, etc. Telegram emphasized banning criminal activities and taking down reported communities. Elliptic co-founder mentioned the shutdown as a significant win against online crime. Another illicit marketplace, Xinbi Guarantee, emerged post-Haowang's closure, with an identified $8.4 billion in transactions so far. Xinbi was linked to a Colorado-based delinquent company, showcasing a robust underground banking system in China via stablecoins for money laundering. US Treasury previously identified Haowang as a money laundering operation. Such black markets present a challenge, with Xinbi's emergence indicating the persistence of illicit activities leveraging crypto payments for money laundering.
news.headline: Telegram has shut down the largest darknet marketplace ever, Haowang Guarantee, facilitating $27B in illicit transactions with USDT stablecoin. Another marketplace, Xinbi Guarantee, has seen $8.4B in transactions and linked to a US company. This move marks a victory against online scams and money laundering.

news.summary: Chainalysis CEO Jonathan Levin emphasized during the 2025 Consensus crypto conference that law enforcement has been successful in tracing stolen funds and crypto ransom payments, resulting in many arrests. There has been a recent increase in crypto-related kidnappings, with two serious attacks in Paris in May alone. One incident involved a 7 million euro kidnapping plot. Last year, a blockchain investigator reported a rise in crypto home invasion thefts in Western Europe. Levin highlighted that the perception of crypto being untraceable has led to these crimes, but law enforcement has been tracking down criminals involved. A total of 22 in-person crypto-related theft incidents have been recorded this year, with potential underreporting due to victims fearing revictimization. Levin hopes that the arrests of crypto robbers serve as a message that such criminal activities are not profitable. He also stressed the importance of cybersecurity measures to prevent thefts in the industry. Despite some successes in tracking down criminals, Levin noted that the situation regarding offline robberies is still concerning. Law enforcement efforts have shown that ransom payments can potentially be recovered, emphasizing the goal of holding criminals accountable rather than solely focusing on money recovery.
news.headline: Chainalysis CEO warns criminals that crypto is traceable, leading to recent Paris attacks. Law enforcement successful in tracing funds, resulting in arrests. Recent attacks prompt France to address security concerns. Levin hopes robbers take note of arrests, stating crypto kidnapping is not profitable. 22 in-person thefts recorded in 2025, down from 28 in 2024, with potential for higher numbers. University of Cambridge study suggests wrench attacks are underreported.

news.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong announced that the firm is actively seeking merger and acquisition opportunities. The company ended the first quarter with $9.9 billion in US dollar resources and also agreed to acquire crypto options trading platform Deribit for $2.9 billion - the largest deal in the crypto industry thus far. This acquisition will allow Coinbase to enter the crypto derivatives market and continue global expansion. Armstrong stated that they are particularly interested in international companies that align with their goals and can boost product development. While rumors swirled about a possible acquisition of Circle, no announcements were made. Coinbase will be the first crypto company to join the S&P 500 on May 19, resulting in significant stock surges, with shares jumping over 30% since May and nearly 50% in the past month.
news.headline: Coinbase gears up for more acquisitions post $2.9B Deribit buy. CEO Armstrong eyes international deals after ending Q1 with $9.9B resources. Stock surges 30% since May. First crypto firm to join S&P 500 on May 19. Mention of potential acquisition of Circle uncertain. Source: Google Finance Magazine.

news.summary: The US Senate is working on passing the bipartisan stablecoin bill, GENIUS Act, by Memorial Day. Key senators, Cynthia Lummis and Kirsten Gillibrand, mentioned the removal of provisions targeting Trump's crypto interests, including memecoins and a stablecoin. Democratic Senators had stalled the bill earlier due to concerns about Trump's personal profit from crypto deals. The bill includes consumer protection and ethics requirements. Coinbase CEO, Brian Armstrong, hopes for a vote next week. The crypto industry pushes for bill passage before the 2026 midterms to avoid an administration hostile to crypto. The legislation must pass before the August congressional recess. Trump aims to sign both crypto bills before the break. Discussions for the bills are ongoing.
news.headline: Senate removes Trump provisions from stablecoin bill, aims to pass GENIUS Act by May 26. Bipartisan support crucial._CRYPTO ensuring bills pass before midterm elections on Nov. 3, 2026. Bill revisions include ethics requirements, focus on stablecoins.

news.summary: Quadient (QDT) has been recognized as a Leader in the SPARK Matrix™: Accounts Receivable Applications, 2025, by the tech advisory firm QKS Group. This is the fourth consecutive year Quadient has received this acknowledgment. The company, listed on Euronext Paris, is renowned for its global automation platform that facilitates secure and sustainable business connections. Quadient's commitment to aiding businesses in advancing digital transformation and streamlining financial processes is evident through this recognition. The firm excels in creating valuable customer interactions and enhancing overall business performance.
news.headline: Quadient named Leader in SPARK Matrix for Accounts Receivable, highlights include AI-powered automation and real-time payments.

news.summary: Deere & Company (NYSE: DE) is set to reveal its second-quarter earnings on May 15, with analysts predicting earnings per share at $5.64, a decrease from $8.53 in the previous year. The company, headquarted in Moline, Illinois, is expected to announce quarterly revenue of $10.79 billion, down from $13.61 billion last year. In the first quarter of 2025, Deere reported a 30% decline in net sales and revenue, totaling $8.51 billion, surpassing the expected $7.70 billion. Deere's shares closed at $497.50 on Wednesday, experiencing a slight 0.2% drop. For more information, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga for updated analyst ratings and details on stock performance.
news.headline: Deere (NYSE: DE) to report Q2 earnings with $5.64 EPS (from $8.53 last year), revenue at $10.79B (from $13.61B).

news.summary: The global biofumigation market, valued at approximately USD 1.2 billion in 2024, is expected to reach nearly USD 2.5 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 7.5% from 2025 to 2034. The market is driven by the increasing global demand for sustainable crop protection, regulatory pressure on synthetic chemicals, and growing awareness of environmental impacts. Biofumigation involves using glucosinolate-rich plants for pest suppression and soil enrichment, with benefits in soil health and productivity. Factors like sustainable agriculture movements, regulatory pushes for chemical alternatives, R&D, and worker/consumer safety drive market growth. Major companies like BASF SE, UPL Group, Isagro USA, Inc., etc., are leading innovation in biofumigation products. The market is driven by applications like soil health improvement, pest control, nematode and disease management. The rise of organic farming practices, regulatory pressure against chemical pesticides, and focus on long-term sustainability are key drivers.
news.headline: Global biofumigation market valued at $1.2B in 2024, projected to reach $2.5B by 2034, growing at 7.5% CAGR. Rising demand for sustainable crop protection and regulatory scrutiny on synthetic chemicals are key drivers. Shift to eco-friendly practices like using glucosinolate-rich plants for pest control is increasing. Key players include BASF, UPL Group, and Marrone Bio Innovations. Biofumigation serves multiple functions such as soil health improvement, pest control, nematode, and disease management.

news.headline: ZWSOFT launches ZWCAD 2026, their flagship CAD software, focusing on speed and ease of use.

news.summary: US Democrat lawmakers sent a letter to Treasury Secretary demanding access to suspicious activity reports on Trump-backed crypto projects, including World Liberty Financial (WLF) and the Official Trump (TRUMP) token filed since 2023. The probe aims to prevent violations of various laws and guard against financial misconduct involving federal officials. They argue WLF could be misused for foreign influence peddling, while concerns are raised over Justin Sun's investment in WLF. The lawmakers also highlight the lack of public disclosure of Trump coin purchasers, potentially enabling bad actors. The probe also includes requests for information on WinRed, America PAC, Elon Musk, and other PACs. This is part of ongoing Democrat-led efforts to investigate Trump's crypto ventures and address concerns about conflicts of interest and insider trading in the industry.
news.headline: Democrats request access to suspicious activity reports on Trump's crypto projects, seek SARs related to World Liberty Financial and the Official Trump token, flag potential money laundering. Probe includes WinRed, PACs, Elon Musk; aims to prevent financial misconduct in federal officials. Trump-owned ventures under scrutiny for possible misuse, foreign influence peddling, lack of disclosure on coin purchasers. Further concerns raised on Trump's ties to Binance and conflicts of interest in crypto regulation. Democrats target Trump's ability to profit from crypto ventures through bills and inquiries. Magazine questions potential conflict of interest and insider trading in Trump's crypto endeavors.

news.summary: Approximately 300 research and development positions are projected to be established in Japan, underscoring Ericsson's recognition of the country's pivotal role in its investment strategy. This move aims to bolster Japan's connectivity network, encompassing various partners, suppliers, and customers worldwide. The R&D endeavors are geared towards fostering innovation and aiding in the prompt advancement of the domestic digital infrastructure.
news.headline: Ericsson to invest in R&D in Japan, aims to create 300 jobs. Supports country's connectivity ecosystem.

news.summary: In Stockholm on May 15, 2025, Metacon celebrated its most successful first quarter ever, achieving significant financial and operational milestones. Their net sales for the quarter reached SEK 50 million, marking a remarkable increase of SEK 42 million from the previous year. This performance demonstrates Metacon's strong growth trajectory and business success.
news.headline: Metacon's Q1 2025 report shows record success with net sales of SEK 50 million, up by SEK 42 million.

news.summary: Renowned economist Peter Schiff, famously critical of Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), has also directed criticism towards Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH). The cryptocurrency market saw a significant reversal in 2022 after the bullish trend of 2021. ETH plummeted from $3,450 in April to the early $1,000s by mid-June. Schiff's bearish prediction on Twitter in May, post on X, projected Ethereum's decline to around $1,000. Within two weeks, ETH dropped to $1,200, aligning with his forecast. Schiff highlighted the warning, stating, "I warned you guys about this less than three weeks ago. If you're still holding Ethereum at $1,200, it's your own fault." The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Peter Schiff doubted Ethereum at $1,200; since then, it surged 116%. Market reversed in 2022, ETH dropped to $1,000s.

news.summary: Investors in the Hashling NFT project have accused founder Jonathan Mills of misappropriating millions of dollars from the project and a Bitcoin mining operation; a court filing alleges he transferred $3 million to holding company Satoshi Labs LLC. The plaintiffs claim they haven't received the promised equity returns or profits from $1.46 million raised in NFT drops. Mills reportedly ghosted them, created a flawed shareholder agreement to control assets, and had majority stakes in Proof of Work Labs, now Satoshi Labs. Despite Mills claiming no money or NFT experience initially, he, along with plaintiff Dustin Steerman, recruited investors and attended NFT events. The plaintiffs seek restitution and a constructive trust over project assets. Mills remains unresponsive to requests for comment.
news.headline: NFT founder accused of embezzling millions from Hashling NFT & Bitcoin project; investors sue for fraud. $3M+ allegedly stolen.

news.summary: Swedish company Sivers Semiconductors AB (STO: SIVE), known for its photonics and wireless technologies, revealed plans for the upcoming 2025 Capital Markets Day (CMD) to be held at Studio VALO in Stockholm on May 27, starting at 2:00 p.m. This event aims to showcase the company's progress and future strategies to investors and stakeholders.
news.headline: Sivers Semiconductors (STO: SIVE) to host Capital Markets Day in Stockholm on May 27, 2025 at Studio VALO from 2:00 p.m.
